arts.ttl: West Ham punish woeful Wolves defence in home win
arts.sum: West Ham United's Jarrod Bowen struck midway through the second half to secure a deserved 2-1 Premier League home win over Wolverhampton Wanderers, whose poor defending cost them again as they languish second-bottom in the standings.

Wolves head coach Gary O'Neil says he is "not concerned" about his future at the club and remains proud of his players after a 2-1 defeat at West Ham he puts down to "a combination of us not doing enough and decisions going West Ham's way."

arts.ttl: Lopetegui dedicates West Ham win to 'special guy' Antonio
arts.sum: West Ham manager Julen Lopetegui shrugs off scrutiny about his future and dedicates the 2-1 win over Wolves to striker Michail Antonio who has had surgery after being involved in a car crash.

arts.ttl: Soucek and Bowen stave off Wolves on emotional night for West Ham
arts.athr: Jacob Steinberg at the London Stadium
arts.sum: Pitted against the incompetence of Wolves’ defending, the mediocrity of West Ham’s performance was not enough to bring about the end of Julen Lopetegui’s unhappy reign. Instead it is likely to be Gary O’Neil looking for a new job after his side were beaten by avoidable goals from Tomas Soucek and Jarrod Bowen. Wolves, who have the worst defensive record in the top flight, were on for a point when Matt Doherty equalised. Their inability to defend is why they are in trouble, though. It allowed West Ham to win it for Michail Antonio. This was bound to be a night of heightened emotions after the horror of Antonio’s car crash on Saturday. Although the forward survived, fans cannot help but wonder if he will ever play again after having surgery on a broken leg and it was difficult to know if the shock would affect West Ham’s focus. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: West Ham 2-1 Wolves: Highlights, man of the match, stats as Bowen nets winner to leave O'Neil on the brink
arts.sum: Jarrod Bowen scores the winning goal to lead West Ham United to a 2-1 victory over Wolves, alleviating some of the pressure building on Julen Lopetegui while leaving Gary O'Neil on the brink of the sack.

arts.ttl: Gary O'Neil pressure grows as Jarrod Bowen piles misery on Wolves before Mario Lemina hits out
arts.sum: WEST HAM 2-1 WOLVES: Jarrod Bowen scored a fine winner to ease pressure on Julen Lopetegui in East London as things go from bad to worse for Wolves as Mario Lemina hits out at own players and staff

arts.ttl: West Ham pay Antonio tribute before Wolves game
arts.sum: West Ham players warmed up in Michail Antonio's No. 9 shirt before they face Wolves on in the Premier League on Monday after the forward was involved in a car crash on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Bowen stunner gives West Ham crucial win as pressure mounts on O'Neil
arts.sum: FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from West Ham’s win over Wolves in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Bowen and Lemina end up on the floor after full-time scuffle!
arts.sum: Things got heated after the full-time whistle between Jarrod Bowen and Mario Lemina.

Matt Doherty had Wolves level for a short period of time, after impressively converting Rayan Ait-Nouri’s cross, but within three minutes Bowen was allowed too much space to restore West Ham’s lead and they held on from there. In the closing stages Ait-Nouri and Nelson Semedo both had efforts blocked as Wolves grew in belief, while Doherty had a number of bites of the cherry, but desperate West Ham defending ensured it remain goalless at the break.
